{"step_by_step_thinking": "Bohler's method is used to measure the angle of the talo-navicular joint and is not related to measuring anatomical dead space. Xenon dilution technique is used to measure lung ventilation but not specifically for dead space. Spirometry measures lung volumes and flow rates but does not specifically measure dead space. The single breath nitrogen test is a method used to estimate anatomical dead space by measuring the nitrogen content in the expired air after a single breath, which aligns with the query.", "answer_choice": "D"}
